subject: opus decay study, lane/g7e commits 6bcf8e1 (decomposition) + 438db11 (raw data)
data_provenance: full commit patches + 47 run logs + both nsys sqlite exports pulled from bw24-g7e:/home/ubuntu/bw24 to /tmp/g7e-decay (collection agent, read-only)

claim_a.claim: 27B spec acceptance decay is CONTENT-TYPE driven, not context-length driven (corrects the HANDOVER 'acceptance decays with ctx' hypothesis)
evidence: raw stdout re-read: ngen256 p1/p2/p3 = 132.4/122.5/102.2 tok/s at acceptance 70.7/73.1/61.4% (N=3 bit-identical, deterministic greedy)

prose control re-read from raw stdout: GPL-text prompts at MATCHED sizes 1847/6262 tok -> acceptance 91.7%/91.2% (-0.5pp over 4400 ctx) while code p2->p3 drops 73.1->61.4% — ctx length moves acceptance <1pp, content type moves it ~12-30pp
per-position windows in their JSONL flat 1.52-2.12 tok/round across pos 6324-6743 (no within-generation decay)
decomposition arithmetic independently recomputed: acc factor 0.898 x round-time factor 0.860 = 0.772 == measured 102.2/132.4; log-shares 41.8%/58.2% vs claimed 41.1%/58.9% (rounding-level agreement)
caveats: prose control is ONE content pair (GPL legalese vs code/agentic); 'content type' is proven as NOT-ctx-length, the finer taxonomy (prose vs code vs agentic difficulty ordering) rests on n=1 per class

claim_b.claim: 59% of the p1->p3 decay is round-time growth, dominated by FA verify cost at long ctx (54us/layer at ctx~60 vs 373us/layer at ctx~6300, +2.56ms/round of a 3.95ms total round delta)
evidence: independent sqlite queries on their nsys exports: p1 fa_decode_f32 n=2512 avg 54.0us; p3 fa_decode_vec_q n=1088 avg 261.8us + fa_decode_vec_q_rows n=368 avg 373.5us — matches the claimed per-instance numbers exactly
per-round recompute: p1 0.43ms (54us x 8 full-attn layers) vs p3 2.99ms (373.5 x 8) -> growth 2.56ms == claim
total round delta independently recomputed from tok/round / tok/s: 23.58 -> 27.42ms = 3.84ms vs claimed 3.95ms (3% — prime-handling rounding, does not change the 59% share materially)
caveats: p1 runs fa_decode_f32 (f32 KV path at tiny ctx) while p3 runs fa_decode_vec_q/_rows (quantized-KV path) — the 54->373us comparison spans a KERNEL change, not just a ctx change; the growth attribution to 'FA verify at long ctx' still holds because the vec_q kernels ARE the long-ctx path, but a per-kernel ctx-scaling curve would be cleaner

G7e silicon (188 SM RTX PRO 6000); local-rig FA shares differ (local 27B round is ~73% b4 MMVQ per graph-spec-stage3-remeasure) — the ranked next-kernel-target (fa rows shared-K multi-row reuse) is a G7e-lane priority, not automatically the local one

verdict: both claims CONFIRMED; the ranked lever (fa_decode_vec_q_rows shared-K reuse, +5-12% p3) is arithmetically consistent with the confirmed 2.56ms/round FA share
label: neutral
